chains.name: Hinge #1: Feeling-thermometer measurement validity for detecting platform-attributable polarization changes
chains.topConclusionText: The measurement-precision critique of feeling thermometers applies symmetrically: if the measure is too noisy to detect platform-attributable effects, it is equally too noisy for null-result experiments to confirm the absence of effects at the 10% threshold.

chains.name: Hinge #4: Field experiments show measurable reductions in affective polarization from deactivating/replacing algorithmic curation
chains.topConclusionText: Piccardi et al. 2025 does not undermine the convergence of null results from algorithm-level manipulations because it tests a different intervention type and its key result loses significance under population reweighting.

chains.name: Hinge #6: Individual-level effects aggregate to population-level changes meeting the 10% counterfactual threshold
chains.topConclusionText: The algorithm-structure interaction hypothesis is contradicted by null results from experiments conducted within the US political context that should capture any such interaction effects.
